message.content:
You are mapping the TEST / QUESTION GENERATION subsystem of an educational platform at /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP.
Read fully and carefully:
- server/src/models/Test.model.ts
- server/src/services/ai.service.ts (~556 lines, read fully)
- server/src/controllers/test.controller.ts (~25k chars, read fully)
- server/src/services/testResult.service.ts
Goal: explain how tests/questions are generated, stored, validated, and whether questions are reused. Specifically:
- Exact Test schema: what is a question? (text, options, correct answer, explanation, difficulty, links to topic/paragraph/node?)
- Where do questions come from? What source material is fed to the LLM? Which model/prompt?
- Is there ANY question bank / reuse of previously generated questions? Or is every test generated fresh per attempt? (This is critical — verify.)
- How is the generated test tied to a roadmap node / topic / KTP? How is "coverage of the topic" ensured, if at all?
- What anti-hallucination / grounding measures exist (e.g. feeding source text, schema validation, answer verification)?
- How are answers checked and results analyzed?
Return the structured map with exact field names and file:line. In evidence quote the Test schema, the generation prompt, and any persistence/reuse logic.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll analyze the test/question generation subsystem. Let me read all the specified files in parallel.
Now let me read the supporting utility files that contain the actual question validation, grading, and content resolution logic, since they're load-bearing for the analysis.

Now I have a complete picture. Let me check the User model's `getAllQuestionHashes` method and the `getBookContent`/`getChapterContent` methods on Subject, plus the roadmap gating, to nail down the reuse/dedup mechanism and topic coverage.

input.description: Find definitions of hash and content helper methods
Find definitions of hash and content helper methods

content.text: I now have everything needed. Let me capture the key reuse logic in the controller once more (lines 27-30, 117-136, 218-224, 267-273) which I have, plus confirm the `findCachedTest` vs the actual cache path used. I have full understanding. Let me compile the structured map.

entities.name: Test (Mongoose model)
fields: bookId: ObjectId (required, indexed) — NOTE: no ref, just an embedded-book id
chapterId: ObjectId (optional, indexed) — chapter scope of the test
questions: [QuestionSchema] (embedded array, validated to be exactly 10)
sourceContentHash: String (required, indexed) — sha256 of {v,profile,n,text} OR a synthetic dailyPackId for solo
relationships: bookId -> Subject.books[]._id (denormalized id, NOT a populate ref)
Each embedded question.relatedContent links back to topicId/chapterId after resolveTopicTitleToId
entities.notes: Compound index {subjectId,bookId,chapterId,sourceContentHash,testProfile} + {createdAt:-1}. Path validator on 'questions' (line 109-122) enforces EXACTLY 10 questions AND each passes validateEntQuestion — this is an ENT-shaped constraint baked into the schema even though regular tests can be 1-50 (the validator would reject non-10-length regular tests at .create() time). Static findCachedTest (line 124) and method getQuestionHashes (line 145, base64 of questionText) are defined but findCachedTest is NOT used by the controller (controller has its own cache filter).

entities.name: Question (embedded QuestionSchema)
fields: questionText: String required, trim, minlength 5, maxlength 2000
correctMatching: Mixed — {L:R} for matching_single, {L:[R,..]} for matching_multiple
relationships: No per-question difficulty field — difficulty is implicit via question NUMBER/type distribution in the ENT prompt (Q1-3 single, 4-5 multiple, 6-7 matching_single, 8 matching_multiple, 9 short, 10 text_input)
entities.notes: There is NO explicit 'difficulty' field anywhere. There is NO link to a roadmap node or KTP node stored on the question. The only grounding link is relatedContent.{pages,topicId,chapterId,topicTitle}.

entities.name: RelatedContent (embedded)
fields: pages: [Number] required (each element required; defaults to [1] when missing)
topicTitle: String (transient — set by LLM, resolved to topicId then DELETED in resolveTopicTitleToId)
relationships: topicId/chapterId -> Subject.books[].chapters[].topics[]._id, resolved from the LLM-returned topicTitle by case-insensitive title match in test.controller.ts resolveTopicTitleToId (line 79-100)
entities.notes: topicTitle is how the LLM signals topic; the server maps title->_id. If no match, topicId stays unset and that question contributes to NO topic in topicsToReview/mastery.

entities.name: MatchingItem (embedded)
entities.notes: ids must be unique within a column (validateMatchingItems, entQuestion.util.ts:263).

entities.name: ITestHistory (embedded in User)
fields: aiFeedback: lazily filled later, not on submit
relationships: User.getAllQuestionHashes(subjectId,bookId) flattens generatedQuestionsHash across a user's history to feed previousQuestions into next generation
entities.notes: This is the dedup source: per-user history of question-text hashes, scoped to subject+book.

entities.name: SoloSession / SoloAttempt
relationships: dailyPackId groups attempts for leaderboard ranking
entities.notes: Solo 'daily pack' is the strongest REUSE path: one Test per (subject,book,chapter,profile,date) is generated once and shared by all users that day (sourceContentHash = synthetic dailyPackId).

flows.name: Generate test (auth: POST /tests/generate)
steps: test.controller.ts:262 generateTest — assertLearnerSubjectAccess + roadmapService.assertKnowledgeMapTestAllowed (gating only, not coverage)
test.controller.ts:267-273 — if NOT forTrial, short-circuit: findLatestTestBySubject(subjectId) returns the newest Test for the WHOLE subject and serves it (HUGE reuse: ignores book/chapter/hash)
test.controller.ts:274 resolveBookContentForAI(dto) — pulls source text from Subject (subjectContent.service.ts:10)
test.controller.ts:276-277 — load user.getAllQuestionHashes(subjectId,bookId) as previousQuestions
test.controller.ts:283-288 aiService.generateTest(contentForAI, previousQuestions, profile, {questionCount})
test.controller.ts:289 resolveTopicTitleToId — map LLM topicTitle -> topicId/chapterId via Subject book topics
test.controller.ts:290 findOrCreateTest — see reuse field; persists Test.create or returns cache
sanitize (line 149) strips answers (sanitizeQuestionForClient) but in dev appends correctAnswerHint + devPrefillValue unless SHOW_TEST_CORRECT_ANSWERS==='false'
flows.aiCalls: One LLM call path in aiService.generateTest (ai.service.ts:60). Model HARDCODED 'gpt-4o-mini' (ai.service.ts:179,465). OpenAI chat/completions, response_format json_object, temperature 0.45 then 0.2 on retry, max_tokens 16000 (ent) or min(12000,1500+n*500) (regular). Up to 2 attempts with self-correcting follow-up messages on parse/length/validation failure.

flows.name: ENT batched generation (>10 questions)
steps: ai.service.ts:76-83 — if profile ent and questionCount>10 (multiple of 10, <=120) -> generateEntBatchedTest

ai.service.ts:319-352 — loops total/10 batches, each calls generateTest(...,'ent') with content padded with a 'series N of M, do not repeat' note (line 333) and accumulates base64 question hashes into prev to discourage repeats across batches
flows.aiCalls: N separate gpt-4o-mini calls (one per 10-question batch); cross-batch dedup is prompt-only via 'avoid repeating meaning' instruction.

flows.name: Solo daily pack (POST /tests/solo/start)
steps: test.controller.ts:362 startSoloTest — build dailyPackId = 'daily-'+sha1(subject|book|chapter|full/chapter|profile|YYYY-MM-DD).slice16 (line 32-49)
test.controller.ts:378-384 — Test.findOne by {subject,book,chapter,profile,sourceContentHash=dailyPackId}; if found, REUSE
test.controller.ts:386-398 — else generate once (empty previousQuestions) and Test.create with sourceContentHash=dailyPackId
ranked daily limit enforced (line 401-411)
flows.aiCalls: At most one gpt-4o-mini call per daily pack key per day; thereafter zero — fully shared across users.

flows.name: Submit + grade (POST /tests/submit)
steps: test.controller.ts:301 submitTest — load Test by id, checkAnswers (line 175)
checkAnswers matches each stored question to a submitted answer BY questionText (line 184), calls gradeAnswer (entQuestion.util.ts:158) per type, counts correct, computes scorePercent
push ITestHistory onto user (line 324) with generatedQuestionsHash; optionally roadmapService.recordTestSubmitted (line 335) updates roadmap node mastery by scorePercent
Breakdown/topicsToReview and AI feedback are LAZY (separate endpoints), see testResult.service.ts
flows.aiCalls: NONE at submit. gradeAnswer is pure deterministic string/set matching. AI feedback (analyzeAnswers) is deferred to GET /ai-explanation.

flows.name: Breakdown + AI feedback (lazy)
steps: testResult.service.ts:73 buildBreakdown — resolveContext loads Subject once (line 177), aligns answers by questionText, accumulates per-topic correct/wrong via relatedContent.topicId, produces topicsToReview sorted by scorePercent
testResult.service.ts:134 buildAiExplanation -> aiService.analyzeAnswers (ai.service.ts:368)
flows.aiCalls: analyzeAnswers is NOT a real LLM call — it is mock/deterministic (delay(800) + template strings, ai.service.ts:379-433). It builds summary text by score bucket and lists mistakes with 'whereToRead' page refs. No model is invoked for feedback despite the name.

input.reuse: Questions are NOT regenerated per attempt — there are several layers of reuse, some surprisingly broad: (1) STRONGEST and most surprising: for non-trial generate (auth and guest), test.controller.ts:267-273 / 218-224 calls findLatestTestBySubject(subjectId) — Test.findOne({subjectId}).sort({createdAt:-1}) — and if ANY prior test exists for that subject it is returned verbatim, ignoring bookId, chapterId, content hash, profile, and questionCount. So most repeat requests serve the newest existing subject-level test rather than generating. (2) Content-hash cache in findOrCreateTest (test.controller.ts:117-136): but this cache lookup is ONLY consulted when useCache = !process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY — i.e. cache is checked only when no API key is set. With a key present, it skips the cache check and Test.create()s a new doc each time (though layer (1) usually short-circuits before this). (3) Solo daily packs (line 378-398): exactly one Test per (subject,book,chapter,profile,date) keyed by synthetic dailyPackId, shared across all users for the day. (4) There is NO durable question bank / question pool: questions live only inside Test docs; the only 'bank' is the cache of whole 10-question Test docs keyed by sourceContentHash = sha256(JSON{v,profile,n,text}). De-duplication of question CONTENT is best-effort and prompt-only: User.getAllQuestionHashes (base64 of questionText, scoped subject+book) is passed as previousQuestions and injected into the prompt as 'avoid repeating the meaning of these' (ai.service.ts:121/147) — no semantic check, just an instruction the LLM may ignore. So: fresh generation only on a true cache/subject miss; otherwise reuse of a whole prior test.
input.aiCost: AI generation cost is incurred at WHOLE-TEST granularity (one gpt-4o-mini chat completion per generated 10-question Test, or N per batch for >10 ENT), NOT per user and NOT per attempt. Cost is shared because generated Tests are persisted and reused: for non-trial flows the newest subject-level test is reused (findLatestTestBySubject), and solo daily packs are generated once per (subject,book,chapter,profile,day) and shared across all users. previousQuestions (per-user history) only changes the prompt, not whether a call happens. The misleadingly-named analyzeAnswers feedback path is a MOCK (template strings, no LLM, ai.service.ts:368-433) so per-result AI feedback currently costs nothing. Grading at submit is deterministic and free. Net: real LLM spend = number of distinct (cache-miss) test generations, amortized across many attempts/users.
problems: BROAD over-reuse bug-smell: test.controller.ts:268-272 (auth) and 219-223 (guest) serve the newest Test for the ENTIRE subject (Test.findOne({subjectId}).sort(createdAt:-1)) on any non-trial request, ignoring bookId/chapterId/profile/questionCount/topicFocus. A learner asking for a chapter-3 regular 30-question test can be handed a stale full-book 10-question ENT test for the same subject. This dwarfs the precise hash cache below it.

Cache is effectively dead when OpenAI is configured: findOrCreateTest only checks the content-hash cache when useCache = !process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY (test.controller.ts:117). In production (key set), every cache MISS at layer (1) creates a brand-new Test doc even for identical content, accumulating duplicate Test documents.

Schema validator hard-codes 10 questions for ALL profiles: Test.model.ts:109-122 rejects any questions array != 10 and runs ENT validateEntQuestion on each. Regular tests with questionCount 1-9 or 11-50 (allowed by ai.service.ts:73-86) would FAIL Mongoose validation on Test.create — latent inconsistency between AI service limits and the persistence model.

'Coverage of the topic' is NOT guaranteed structurally. There is no enumeration of topics/paragraphs/KTP nodes to ensure each is tested. Coverage is purely a prompt nudge: topicFocus 'all 10 questions should focus on topic X, and if not enough material, generate the closest in meaning' (ai.service.ts:100-104) — which actively invites off-source questions. Topic linkage is reverse-engineered after the fact via case-insensitive title match (resolveTopicTitleToId), and unmatched titles are silently dropped (topicId unset), so those questions never count toward topicsToReview or roadmap mastery.

Anti-hallucination is weak: the only grounding is dumping raw concatenated paragraph text into the user message (Subject.getBookContent/getChapterContent join paragraph.content.text). There is NO retrieval, NO citation verification, NO check that questionText/answers actually appear in the source, and NO answer-correctness verification. relatedContent.pages defaults to [1] whenever the model omits or fabricates pages (ai.service.ts:307-309, entQuestion.util.ts:292), so page references are unreliable.

Question-text matching for grading/answer-lookup is by exact questionText string (checkAnswers test.controller.ts:184; testResult byText map). If two questions share identical text, or a client mutates whitespace, the lookup is ambiguous/fails; dedup hashes are also raw base64(questionText) with no normalization (User.model.ts:239) so trivial rephrasings defeat dedup.

Misleading naming / dead-ish code: analyzeAnswers (ai.service.ts:368) is documented as AI feedback and lives in the AI service but is a pure mock with an artificial delay(800); rephraseQuestion (line 441) and isQuestionDuplicate/hashQuestion (line 507-517) appear unused by the generation path. The file header still says 'AI SERVICE (MOCK)' though generateTest is now a real OpenAI call.

No semantic dedup across attempts/batches: previousQuestions and cross-batch 'don't repeat' are prompt-only; nothing prevents the model from re-emitting near-duplicate questions, and the per-user hash scope (subject+book) misses chapter-level repetition nuance.
aiExplanation is silently backfilled with a generic placeholder string when the model omits it (pickAiExplanation, entQuestion.util.ts:271-278), so a 'required' explanation can be content-free.
evidence: Test schema (Test.model.ts:71-104): subjectId ref Subject required index; bookId ObjectId required index; chapterId ObjectId index; questions: [QuestionSchema]; sourceContentHash: String required index; testProfile enum ['regular','ent'] default 'ent'; timestamps; collection 'tests'.

Question schema (Test.model.ts:32-67): questionType enum [...ENT_QUESTION_TYPES] default 'single_choice'; questionText required min 5 max 2000; options [String]; correctOption String; correctOptions [String]; matchingLeft/matchingRight [MatchingItemSchema]; correctMatching Mixed; acceptableAnswers/acceptableKeywords [String]; referenceAnswer String; aiExplanation required max 1200; relatedContent RelatedContentSchema required. (No 'difficulty' field, no roadmapNode field.)

10-question hard validator (Test.model.ts:109-122): "if (!Array.isArray(questions) || questions.length !== 10) return false; ... validateEntQuestion(q, i) ... 'Тест должен содержать ровно 10 корректных вопросов в формате ЕНТ'".

Model + call params (ai.service.ts:464-470): "model: 'gpt-4o-mini', temperature, max_tokens, response_format: { type: 'json_object' }, messages". generateTest logs model: 'gpt-4o-mini' (ai.service.ts:179).

ENT generation prompt core (ai.service.ts:128-150): "Ты — эксперт по подготовке к ЕНТ (Казахстан). Сгенерируй ровно 10 вопросов... РАСПРЕДЕЛЕНИЕ ПО ТИПАМ: 1–3 single_choice ... 4–5 multiple_choice ... 6–7 matching_single ... 8 matching_multiple ... 9 short_answer ... 10 text_input ... relatedContent: { pages, topicTitle? }".

Source text fed to LLM (ai.service.ts:153-164): prompt = [specBody, 'Требования:', languageRule, topicFocusHint, 'Контент для генерации:', content.text]; content.text comes from Subject.getBookContent/getChapterContent which join paragraph.content.text (Subject.model.ts:168-194).

Subject-level over-reuse (test.controller.ts:28-30): "private async findLatestTestBySubject(subjectId) { return Test.findOne({ subjectId }).sort({ createdAt: -1 }); }" and used at line 268-272: "if (!dto.forTrial) { const cached = await this.findLatestTestBySubject(dto.subjectId); if (cached) { success(res, this.sanitize(cached), 'Test loaded from last saved (same subject)', 201); return; } }".

Cache only without API key (test.controller.ts:117): "const useCache = !process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY; if (useCache) { ... const cached = await Test.findOne(cacheFilter)...; if (cached) return cached; } return Test.create({ ... sourceContentHash: generatedTest.sourceContentHash, testProfile });".

Per-user prompt-only dedup (User.model.ts:233-240): getAllQuestionHashes filters testHistory by subjectId+bookId and flatMaps generatedQuestionsHash; injected at test.controller.ts:277 and rendered into prompt as 'Избегай повторения смысла этих вопросов' (ai.service.ts:121,147).

Solo daily pack reuse key (test.controller.ts:32-49 buildDailyPackId = 'daily-'+sha1(subject|book|chapter|full|profile|dateKey).slice(0,16)) used as sourceContentHash for a shared Test (line 378-398).

Deterministic grading (entQuestion.util.ts:158-228 gradeAnswer): single_choice normText equality; multiple_choice setsEqualStrings; matching JSON object compare; short_answer/text_input normText includes + keyword 60% threshold + referenceAnswer substring. checkAnswers matches by questionText (test.controller.ts:184).
Mock 'AI' feedback (ai.service.ts:379-433): "await this.delay(800); ... scorePercent ... summary = `Отличная работа!...` ... return { summary, mistakes };" — no model call.

topicFocus invites off-source content (ai.service.ts:102-103): "Все 10 вопросов должны быть сфокусированы на теме «...». Если в контенте недостаточно материала именно по этой теме, генерируй максимально близкие по смыслу вопросы."

Topic linkage resolved post-hoc by title match, title dropped (test.controller.ts:79-99 resolveTopicTitleToId): finds topic where title.toLowerCase()===topicTitle.toLowerCase(), sets relatedContent.topicId/chapterId, then delete q.relatedContent.topicTitle.

content.text:
I've completed the analysis of the test/question generation subsystem. The structured map has been returned. Key findings summary:

**Schema**: A `Test` (collection `tests`) embeds exactly 10 `questions` plus `subjectId`/`bookId`/`chapterId`/`sourceContentHash`/`testProfile`. A question has `questionType` (6 ENT types), `questionText`, type-specific answer fields (`options`+`correctOption`, `correctOptions`, `matchingLeft/Right`+`correctMatching`, `acceptableAnswers/Keywords`, `referenceAnswer`), a required `aiExplanation`, and `relatedContent` (pages + topicId/chapterId/topicTitle). **No `difficulty` field and no roadmap-node link** exist — difficulty is implicit in the Q1-10 type distribution.

**Source/model**: Raw concatenated `paragraph.content.text` from the Subject book/chapter is dumped into the user prompt. Model is hardcoded `gpt-4o-mini`, `response_format: json_object`, temp 0.45→0.2, up to 2 self-correcting attempts.

**Reuse (the critical question)**: Questions are NOT generated fresh per attempt. There is no durable question bank — the only "bank" is whole cached `Test` docs. Three reuse layers, the broadest of which looks like a bug: for any non-trial request the controller serves `Test.findOne({subjectId}).sort({createdAt:-1})` — the newest test for the entire subject, ignoring book/chapter/profile/questionCount. The precise content-hash cache below it is only consulted when no OpenAI key is set. Solo daily packs are generated once per (subject,book,chapter,profile,day) and shared across all users. Cross-test/question dedup is prompt-only (base64 question-text hashes injected as "avoid repeating").

**Coverage & grounding**: No structural topic coverage guarantee — purely a `topicFocus` prompt nudge that even invites off-source questions. No retrieval, citation verification, or answer-correctness checking; `relatedContent.pages` defaults to `[1]` when omitted. Topic linkage is reverse-mapped by case-insensitive title match after generation, with unmatched titles silently dropped.

**Grading/results**: Deterministic per-type string/set matching in `gradeAnswer`; submit is AI-free. The `analyzeAnswers` "AI feedback" is actually a mock (template strings + artificial delay), so per-result feedback currently costs nothing.
